title: VOA — Overcommitment, Everest, Brain Health, Asteroid Defense
content: Section 1: 'spreading ourself too thin' idioms · Section 2: Nepal raises Everest permits · Section 3: dementia prevention · Section 4: planetary asteroid defence
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: The main idiom of the lesson is 'to ________ ourselves too thin', meaning to take on too much.
A common synonym is 'to bite off more than we can ________'.
According to the host, what often happens when we work on too many projects at once?
options: A. We finish them faster than expected
B. None of them is done well
C. We become better at multitasking
D. We feel calmer and more in control
questions.answer: B. None of them is done well

questions.question: Performing several tasks at the same time is called ________.
Someone who is obligated beyond their ability to fulfil their duties is described as ________.
Which word does the host use to describe being 'overcome by force, number, or feeling'?
In the closing dialogue, the students were rehearsing a school ________ at night.
The students were also working at part-time ________ outside school.
What does the teacher in the dialogue decide to do?
questions.answer: A. Extend the deadline and warn the students about overcommitment
options: A. Extend the deadline and warn the students about overcommitment
C. Give the students extra homework

questions.question: In this lesson, the expression 'pushed to the limit' is closest in meaning to which idiom?
Mount Everest is ________ metres high.
What is the new cost of a spring-season permit to climb Everest?
What was the previous price of the spring-season permit before the increase?
The new autumn-season permit fee is ________ dollars.
The previous permit price had been unchanged for about ________ years.
How many of the world's 14 mountains above 8,000 metres are located in Nepal?
More than ________ climbers bought Everest permits last year.
Last year's permit revenue brought in about ________ million dollars.
What two things will the extra money be used for, according to the tourism director?
options: A. Building new hotels and roads
B. Waste removal and search-and-rescue operations
D. Lowering prices for local climbers
questions.answer: B. Waste removal and search-and-rescue operations

questions.question: What new requirement is mentioned for climbers under the updated rules?
options: A. They must hire two Sherpas each
B. They must carry GPS trackers and bags for human waste
C. They must climb only in groups of ten
D. They must complete a Nepali language course
questions.answer: B. They must carry GPS trackers and bags for human waste

questions.question: The health condition the study focuses on is ________.
The study was published in the journal Nature ________.
About how many Americans are predicted to develop dementia each year by 2060?
What is the lifetime risk of dementia for people aged 55 and older, according to the study?
Only ________ percent of people develop dementia between the ages of 55 and 75.
What is the average lifetime risk of dementia for women in the study?
Which is identified as a major genetic risk factor for dementia?
The most common form of dementia is ________ disease.
Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a way to lower the risk of dementia?
options: B. Staying socially and cognitively active

questions.question: Why is using hearing aids recommended in the feature?
options: A. It helps people stay socially and cognitively active, lowering dementia risk
B. It directly cures early-stage dementia
C. It replaces the need for memory training
D. It is required by U.S. law for seniors
questions.answer: A. It helps people stay socially and cognitively active, lowering dementia risk

questions.question: The asteroid discussed in the feature is named 2024 ________.
What is the estimated diameter of asteroid 2024 YR4?
NASA estimated a ________ percent chance the asteroid would hit Earth.
The possible impact date discussed is 22 December ________.
What was NASA's DART mission designed to do?
options: B. Change an asteroid's orbit by hitting it with a spacecraft
C. Land humans on an asteroid
questions.answer: B. Change an asteroid's orbit by hitting it with a spacecraft

questions.question: The asteroid that DART successfully struck was named ________.
How does a 'gravity tractor' work as a deflection method?
options: A. A spacecraft flies alongside the asteroid and slowly pulls it off course
B. A satellite drops a heavy weight onto the asteroid
C. A laser melts the asteroid in place
D. A magnet pulls the asteroid toward Earth
questions.answer: A. A spacecraft flies alongside the asteroid and slowly pulls it off course

questions.question: Why might painting one side of an asteroid white help deflect it?
options: A. The bright colour makes it easier to track
B. Reflected sunlight creates a small push that slowly changes its course
C. It heats the rock and breaks it apart
D. It sends a signal to nearby satellites
questions.answer: B. Reflected sunlight creates a small push that slowly changes its course

questions.question: Nuclear weapons are considered most useful only for asteroids on the ________ scale (about 1,000 metres or more).
Experts say humanity has a preparation window of about ________ years before the possible impact.
